subtitle: In the shadow of fallen kingdoms, two brothers shape stone, river and memory into Vijayanagara, a City of Victory whose birth bridges politics, inscriptions and sacred story.

panels.shot: epic wide of rocky Hampi landscape at dawn
panels.action: Harihara I and Bukka Raya I stand side by side on a granite height above the Tungabhadra valley, surveying scattered shrines and boulders that will become the city of Vijayanagara.

dialogue.text: In the fourteenth century, Harihara and Bukka, sons of Sangama, raise Vijayanagara, a City of Victory born from river, rock and the long memory of Karnataka’s shattered realms.

i18n.hi: चौदहवीं सदी में संगम के बेटे हरिहर और बुक्का उठाते हैं विजयनगर — नदी, चट्टान और कर्नाटक के टूटे राजों की लंबी यादों से जन्मी विजय की नगरी।

panels.shot: slow aerial glide over ruins of Kampili on the Tungabhadra
panels.action: Broken fort walls and silent gateways of Kampili lie above the river, fields returning through stone, hinting at a once proud but now fallen local kingdom.

dialogue.text: Before Vijayanagara, there was Kampili on this river, a small kingdom in the Tungabhadra valley whose proud walls could not withstand the fierce storms of the early Delhi Sultanate.

i18n.hi: विजयनगर से पहले इसी नदी पर था कम्पिलि — तुंगभद्रा घाटी का छोटा राज्य, जिसकी गर्वीली दीवारें शुरुआती दिल्ली सल्तनत के तूफानी हमले झेल न सकीं।

panels.shot: montage wide of Hoysala temples and fort-towns
panels.action: Intricate Hoysala temples rise amidst town walls and forested hills, showing a network of local powers, markets and shrines woven across Karnataka.

dialogue.text: Around Kampili stretched the larger Hoysala world: temple cities, fort-towns, markets and overlords in shifting alliances, ruling a land where stone shrines and royal banners shared the same sky.

i18n.hi: कम्पिलि के चारों ओर फैला था बड़ा होयसला जगत — मंदिर नगर, किले-नगरी, बाज़ार और सरदारों की बदलती सांठ-गांठ; जहाँ पत्थर के देवालय और राज-ध्वज एक ही आसमान बाँटते थे।

panels.shot: somber wide of a Deccan battlefield aftermath
panels.action: A hazy plain under a harsh sun shows abandoned war gear and distant riders, suggesting recent conflict without graphic detail, with hills of the Deccan in the background.

dialogue.text: The early 1300s tore through this region. Armies from Delhi pushed south, campaigns struck Devagiri, Dwarasamudra and Kampili. Old centers cracked, and lesser chiefs rose or fell in dust and silence.

i18n.hi: तेरह सौ की शुरुआत ने इस धरती को चीर दिया। दिल्ली से सेनाएँ दक्षिण उतरीं, देवगिरि, द्वारसमुद्र और कम्पिलि पर चढ़ाईं हुईं। पुराने केंद्र टूटे, छोटे सरदार धूल और ख़ामोशी में उठे-गिरे।

panels.shot: medium shot of the brothers in simple warrior dress
panels.action: Harihara and Bukka stand amid a modest hall, dressed as regional commanders, conferring over a rough map of rivers and forts drawn on palm-leaf and cloth.

dialogue.text: From this broken landscape emerged two brothers, Harihara and Bukka, rooted in Kampili and Hoysala service, men of the frontier whose story later chronicles would wrap in legend.

i18n.hi: इसी टूटे भूगोल से उभरे दो भाई — हरिहर और बुक्का, कम्पिलि और होयसला सेवा में जड़ें जमाए, सरहद के जाँबाज़ जिनकी कहानी को बाद की कथाएँ दंतकथा में लपेट देंगी।

panels.shot: close three-quarter view of Harihara looking out of a fort window
panels.action: Harihara gazes through a stone lattice toward the Tungabhadra valley, his face thoughtful, while Bukka stands slightly behind, attentive, suggesting shared but uncertain purpose.

dialogue.text: Traditions say they served, were captured, even converted under northern power, then returned to their homeland. Historians see not a simple tale, but the hard play of border politics.

i18n.hi: कहावतें कहती हैं वे सेवा में थे, पकड़े गए, उत्तरी ताकत के नीचे बदले भी, फिर लौट आए अपनी धरती पर। इतिहासकारों को इसमें सीधी कथा नहीं, सरहद की सख्त सियासत दिखती है।

panels.shot: serene wide of Sringeri monastery on the Tunga river
panels.action: The Sringeri matha sits peacefully by the river Tunga, ascetics and students moving quietly among tiled roofs and pillared halls, with green hills behind.

dialogue.text: To the west, Sringeri’s matha watched these changes from the Tunga river. It was a world of teachers, rituals and manuscripts, whose blessings would later be woven into royal origin stories.

i18n.hi: पश्चिम में तुङ्गा नदी किनारे श्रीङ्गेरी का मठ यह सब देख रहा था — गुरुओं, विधियों और पाण्डुलिपियों की दुनिया, जिसकी कृपा बाद में राजवंश की जन्म-कहानी में बुनी जाएगी।

panels.shot: wide shot of the brothers riding toward the Hampi boulder country
panels.action: Harihara and Bukka ride with a small escort through a landscape of massive rounded boulders and scrub, the Tungabhadra river glinting in the distance and scattered shrines dotting the rocks.

dialogue.text: In this swirl of powers, the brothers turned their eyes to the boulder-ringed valley near Hampi, where river, hill and scattered shrines offered shelter, resources and an ancient sacred feel.

i18n.hi: इन ताकतों की हलचल में भाइयों की नज़र गई हंपी के पास चट्टानों से घिरी घाटी पर — जहाँ नदी, पहाड़ी और बिखरे देवस्थानों ने आसरा, संसाधन और पुरातन पावनता दी।

panels.shot: low-angle wide of the brothers standing by the Tungabhadra bank
panels.action: On a rocky riverbank, Harihara stands with arms folded, Bukka beside him, as they survey ferries, farmers and simple shrines, sensing a future capital in this raw landscape.

dialogue.speaker: harihara_i_and_bukka_raya_i
dialogue.text: We need a seat that stones can defend and stories can bless.

i18n.hi: हमें ऐसा आसन चाहिए जिसे पत्थर बचाएँ और कथाएँ आशीर्वाद दें।

panels.shot: delicate close-up of an inscription being carved
panels.action: A sculptor’s hand chisels neat Kannada letters into a stone slab, with partially carved lines referencing Harihara’s rule and a place-name linked to the emerging capital.

dialogue.text: In 1346, a Sringeri grant inscribed in stone speaks clearly: Harihara, already a ruler, already tied to this region, supporting Sringeri and naming an early capital with pride.

i18n.hi: १३४६ में पत्थर पर खुदा श्रीङ्गेरी का दान साफ बोलता है — हरिहर तब तक शासक, इस भू से बँधे, श्रीङ्गेरी के संरक्षक और पहली राजधानी का नाम गर्व से लिखवाते हुए।

panels.shot: interior wide of a council in a simple hall at Hampi
panels.action: Harihara and Bukka sit at the head of a stone-pillared hall, listening as local chiefs and merchants bow and speak, maps and revenue records spread before them.

dialogue.text: Power for them did not come in a single thunderclap of 1336. It grew step by step: alliances with local chiefs, control of roads and fields, temple lands and tax rights in the river valley.

i18n.hi: उनके लिए ताकत एक ही १३३६ की गर्जना से नहीं आई। वह कदम-कदम से बढ़ी — स्थानीय नायकों से गठजोड़, राहों और खेतों पर पकड़, मंदिर भूमियों और नदी घाटी के कर-अधिकार पर काबू।

panels.shot: wide of temple construction scene amid boulders
panels.action: Artisans raise stone pillars, carve deities and haul beams as a new temple complex rises against the granite hills, blending royal architecture into the older sacred landscape.

dialogue.text: Temples rose alongside walls. By placing shrines, halls and processional streets, the new rulers stitched royal authority into older sacred ground, turning rough hills into a living capital.

i18n.hi: किलों के साथ मंदिर उठे। नए राजाओं ने देवालय, मंडप और शोभायात्रा की गलियाँ सजाकर पुराने पावन स्थल में राजसत्ता पिरो दी, ऊबड़ पहाड़ियों को धड़कती राजधानी में बदल दिया।

panels.shot: medium shot inside Sringeri of an elder reading a copperplate grant
panels.action: An elder at Sringeri studies a freshly issued copperplate grant, its shining surface engraved with royal names, place names and gifts of revenue from the emerging Vijayanagara realm.

dialogue.text: Grants flowed to Sringeri and other centers. Later tradition would place Vidyaranya as the guiding sage of the brothers, but inscriptions show his rise to full pontiff only in following years.

i18n.hi: दानों की धार श्रीङ्गेरी और अन्य केंद्रों तक पहुँची। बाद की परंपरा विद्यारण्य को भाइयों का मार्गदर्शक ऋषि बनाएगी, पर शिलालेख दिखाते हैं कि उनका पूर्ण पीठाधिपति बनना आगे के सालों में हुआ।

panels.shot: close-up on Harihara and Bukka in conversation under a stone mandapa
panels.action: Harihara and Bukka sit under a small mandapa, dusk light soft on their faces, discussing the balance between military strength, temple patronage and alliances with religious centers.

dialogue.speaker: harihara_i_and_bukka_raya_i
dialogue.text: Let grants, not just swords, bind this valley to our house.

i18n.hi: तलवार से नहीं, दानों से भी इस घाटी को अपने घर से बाँधें।

panels.shot: expansive bird’s-eye of the growing cityscape of Vijayanagara
panels.action: A sweeping view shows fortified suburbs, temple spires, markets and river ghats spreading between granite hills, capturing Vijayanagara’s early emergence as a major city.

dialogue.text: By mid-century, the valley had changed. The City of Victory spread across hill and river, its streets and courts drawing traders, soldiers and pilgrims from across Karnataka and beyond.

i18n.hi: मध्य सदी तक घाटी बदल चुकी थी। विजय की नगरी पहाड़ और नदी पर फैल गई; उसकी गलियाँ और दरबार कर्नाटक और दूर-दूर से व्यापारी, सैनिक और यात्री खींचने लगे।

panels.shot: symbolic tableau of four royal emblems set against Vijayanagara walls
panels.action: Against the backdrop of Vijayanagara’s massive walls, stylized emblems representing the Sangama, Saluva, Tuluva and Aravidu houses are arrayed, hinting at future dynastic sequences.

dialogue.text: From Harihara and Bukka’s Sangama line would later come Saluva, Tuluva and Aravidu dynasties. Each would claim this city as theirs, rewriting origins to suit new times and new needs.

i18n.hi: हरिहर और बुक्का की संगम रेखा से आगे चलकर सालुव, तुळुव और अरविदु वंश आएँगे। हर वंश इस नगर को अपना बताकर नई जरूरतों के हिसाब से जन्म-कथाएँ फिर से लिखेगा।

panels.shot: balanced wide of Vijayanagara and a distant Islamic-style fort on another hill
panels.action: Vijayanagara’s temples and ramparts fill one side of the frame, while, far across the plain, an Islamic-style fort and minaret rise, both under the same sky, separated by distance, not caricature.

dialogue.text: Later storytellers cast Vijayanagara as a pure bulwark against Islam. Yet its birth lay in shared borderlands, negotiations and rivalries, not in a single, simple religious wall.

i18n.hi: बाद के किस्सागो विजयनगर को इस्लाम के खिलाफ शुद्ध किले की तरह दिखाते हैं। पर इसका जन्म साझा सरहदों, सौदे और टकरावों में हुआ, किसी एक साधी धार्मिक दीवार में नहीं।

panels.shot: intimate close-up of an old palm-leaf manuscript next to a stone inscription
panels.action: A palm-leaf manuscript with flowing narrative script lies beside a weathered stone inscription, their different textures lit by a single shaft of light in a dark archive-like space.

dialogue.text: Manuscripts speak of miraculous reconversions and sage-guided kings. Stones speak of grants, dates, places and rulers. Together they show how memory and evidence entwine in the city’s origin.

i18n.hi: पाण्डुलिपियाँ चमत्कारी पुनः-धर्म परिवर्तन और ऋषि-मार्गदर्शित राजाओं की बात करती हैं। पत्थर दान, तारीख, स्थान और शासक दर्ज करते हैं। दोनों मिलकर दिखाते हैं कि स्मृति और सबूत कैसे नगर की शुरुआत में गुँथ जाते हैं।

panels.shot: heroic low-angle of the brothers atop a Hampi hill at sunset
panels.action: At sunset, Harihara and Bukka stand on a high boulder above the completed early city, silhouettes strong against the glowing sky, the Tungabhadra winding below through temples and streets.

dialogue.text: Today we remember Vijayanagara through both legend and stone. Its founders teach that great beginnings endure best when epic stories are tested against inscriptions, dates and the quiet weight of evidence.

i18n.hi: आज हम विजयनगर को कथा और पत्थर दोनों से याद करते हैं। इसके संस्थापक सिखाते हैं कि महान शुरुआत तब तक टिकती है जब तक महाकाव्य कथाएँ शिलालेखों, तारीखों और साक्ष्य की शांत भारीपन से परखी जाती हैं।

title_i18n.en: Harihara and Bukka Raise a City of Victory
title_i18n.hi: हरिहर और बुक्का ने रची विजय की नगरी

subtitle_i18n.en: In the shadow of fallen kingdoms, two brothers shape stone, river and memory into Vijayanagara, a City of Victory whose birth bridges politics, inscriptions and sacred story.
subtitle_i18n.hi: गिरे राज्यों की छाया में दो भाई पत्थर, नदी और यादों से गढ़ते हैं विजयनगर — विजय की नगरी जहाँ जन्म में ही राज, शिलालेख और पावन कथाएँ जुड़ जाती हैं।
